tests.title: NTC thermistor — resistance reference
tests.summary: Typical resistance values for 10k NTC thermistors used in most domestic boilers. Measure with the sensor unplugged from the PCB.
safetyWarnings: Isolate power before unplugging sensor connectors

procedure.body: Isolate power and unplug the NTC from the PCB. Note which terminals connect to the sensor.
Measure resistance across the two sensor pins at current pipe/room temperature.
procedure.expected: Within ±15% of the value in the table above for the measured temperature

procedure.body: Sensor is within tolerance. Check the wiring back to the PCB next.
NTC has gone open circuit. Replace the sensor.
NTC has shorted. Replace the sensor.
NTC has drifted out of range. Replace the sensor.

tests.title: Gas valve coil — resistance check
tests.summary: Quick test to decide whether a gas valve coil has failed open or shorted. Always refer to the service manual for the exact expected value for your model.
safetyWarnings: Isolate electrical supply before disconnecting the gas valve plug
Close the gas isolation valve before doing any further gas valve work

procedure.body: Turn off the electrical supply and unplug the gas valve connector from the harness.
Measure resistance across the two coil terminals.
procedure.expected: Typically 1–4 kΩ for modulating coils; confirm against the service manual

procedure.body: Coil is healthy. If the valve still isn't opening on call for heat, confirm the PCB is delivering 230V to the valve at the right moment.
Coil open circuit — gas valve must be replaced.
Coil short circuit — gas valve must be replaced.

tests.title: Fan motor — winding resistance
tests.summary: Check whether a fan motor has an open or shorted winding before condemning it.
safetyWarnings: Isolate mains power before unplugging the fan

procedure.body: Isolate power and unplug the fan supply/tacho connector.
Measure resistance across the two supply (motor) pins. Different manufacturers use different ranges.
procedure.expected: Typically 10–80 Ω — refer to the service manual for the exact figure

procedure.body: Motor winding is healthy. Spin the fan by hand — any grinding or stiffness indicates worn bearings.
If the fan spins smoothly and resistance is in range but the boiler still reports a fan fault, the problem is likely the tacho signal or the PCB fan driver. Verify tacho continuity next.
Winding open — fan assembly needs replacing.
Winding shorted — fan assembly needs replacing.

tests.title: Flame rectification current — µA reading
tests.summary: Good flame-sense electrodes produce a small DC current when the burner is lit. A drifted or dirty electrode will show a low reading. Use a multimeter capable of reading DC µA or use the boiler's service menu if it displays ionisation.
safetyWarnings: Mains voltage and live burner — only break into the flame-sense lead with proper test adaptors

procedure.body: If the boiler reports ionisation current in its diagnostic menu, use that — it's safer than breaking into the lead. Put the boiler into high-rate service test and read the value.
Note the reading at high rate.
procedure.expected: Generally 4 µA or more on a healthy burner

procedure.body: Flame-sense is healthy. If the boiler is still reporting flame faults, suspect the lead or PCB.
Clean the flame-sense electrode (fine abrasive, not wire wool on the ceramic) and re-test. If still low, replace the electrode.
